2605.30036.title: Teaching Values to Machines: Simulating Human-Like Behavior in LLMs
2605.30036.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate a remarkable capacity to adopt different personas and roles; however, it remains unclear whether they can manifest behavior that adheres to a coherent, human-like value structure. In this work, we draw on established psychological value theory to induce human-like values in LLMs and assess their alignment with patterns observed in human studies. Using validated psychological questionnaires, we conduct large-scale experiments -- over 5 million questions -- to evaluate value structures and value-behavior relationships in leading LLMs and compare them to humans. Our findings reveal strong agreement between value-prompted LLMs and humans across both dimensions. Moreover, incorporating human value distributions enhances population-level simulations with value-induced LLMs. These findings highlight the potential of value-induced LLMs as effective, psychologically grounded tools for simulating human behavior.
2605.30036.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）展現出採取不同角色和人格的卓越能力；然而，尚不清楚它們是否能表現出符合一致的人類價值結構的行為。<lb/>在本研究中，我們基於既有的心理價值理論，誘導LLMs中的人類價值，並評估它們與人類研究中觀察到的模式的一致性。<lb/>我們使用經過驗證的心理問卷，進行大規模實驗——超過500萬個問題——以評估領先LLMs中的價值結構和價值-行為關係，並將其與人類進行比較。<lb/>我們的研究結果顯示，價值引導的LLMs與人類在這兩個維度上有著強烈的一致性。<lb/>此外，結合人類價值分佈增強了使用價值誘導的LLMs進行的群體級模擬。<lb/>這些發現突顯了價值誘導的LLMs作為有效且具有心理基礎的工具來模擬人類行為的潛力。
2605.30036.author: Asaf Yehudai et.al.
2605.30036.authors: Asaf Yehudai, Naama Rozen, Ariel Gera
